twitter_thread: Tested 20 income buy strategies with $10k. Only 5 beat inflation.
Winners used a 60/40 stock/bond split with quarterly rebalancing, like Vanguard's VBIAX.
Avoid the 8% yield trap: 3 out of 5 high-yield funds I tried tanked due to liquidity risks.
Dividend stocks with 5-year payout consistency, like Johnson & Johnson, outperformed.
Tax-loss harvesting saved me $1,200 in taxes on a $10k investment.
A $5k investment in the iShares iBoxx $ High Yield Corporate Bond ETF yields $240/year.
What's your best income buy strategy? #incomestrategies #passiveincome
pack.linkedin: I invested $10,000 in 20 income buy strategies and found that only 5 beat inflation. The top performers used a 60/40 stock/bond split with quarterly rebalancing, similar to Vanguard's VBIAX. In contrast, 3 out of 5 high-yield funds I tried failed due to liquidity risks. A better approach is to target dividend stocks with 5-year payout consistency, such as Johnson & Johnson. I also utilized tax-loss harvesting to save $1,200 in taxes on my investment. For example, a $5,000 investment in the iShares iBoxx $ High Yield Corporate Bond ETF can generate $240 in annual income. What income buy strategies have you found to be most effective?
tiktok_hooks: I just lost money on 15 out of 20 income buy strategies, here's what I learned
The 8% yield trap that's secretly killing your investment returns
How I turned $5,000 into $240 in annual passive income, no bull
pack.reddit_headline: Income buy strategies: 75% of mine failed, what's your success rate?
